description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese torii gate, typically found at the entrance to Shinto shrines, accompanied by a small stone lantern. The torii features two tall, cylindrical red pillars with gray bases, supporting a gently curved, dark blue crossbeam and roof-like top. A horizontal support beam connects the two pillars, and a secondary crossbar sits just below the main beam. The stone lantern, positioned beside the torii, has a square base, a red central shaft, and a blue-tiled roof with a golden finial. The overall design is minimalist yet iconic, with clean lines and bold color contrasts. The torii and lantern are both constructed from materials that evoke wood and stone, reflecting traditional Japanese craftsmanship.
description: A striking red Japanese torii gate with a curved blue roof and gray bases, accompanied by a small stone lantern with a blue-tiled roof and gold finial.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the torii gate and the lantern on the left side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the torii's roof and the lantern directly behind the left pillar.
description_view_2: Rear view of the torii gate with the lantern now on the right side, mirroring the front view.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the torii's roof profile and the lantern behind the right pillar.
